Event: Quinn and Kurt secretly help Sam at the motel
Explanation: They support Sam as good friends, helping him without evident harm to themselves or others.

Event: Sam dates multiple girls and stays close friends with them afterward
Explanation: He gains romantic experience and lasting friendships while they retain a positive relationship with him.

Event: Santana briefly dates Sam to hide that she’s a lesbian
Explanation: She gains cover and safety while deceiving him and limiting his informed choice in the relationship.

Event: Sam protects his reputation by reacting strongly if accused of cheating or selling himself
Explanation: Defending boundaries protects his social standing, with at most minor social friction toward others.

Event: Sam refuses to tolerate attacks on anyone in New Directions
Explanation: He risks conflict but clearly benefits and protects his friends and group cohesion.

Event: Sam struggles with whether to embrace a “brainless beauty” image
Explanation: Trying to fit a shallow image undermines his self-concept without concrete benefit shown.

Event: Artie mocks Sam’s “Bieber cut” at their first meeting
Explanation: Artie gains a brief social laugh at Sam’s expense, offering Sam no clear benefit.

Event: Sam sings Justin Bieber songs in “Comeback”
Explanation: He gains performance time and likely praise while entertaining others.

Event: Sam declares publicly he will fight to win Mercedes back despite her boyfriend
Explanation: He pursues his desires at the cost of awkwardness and pressure for Mercedes and disrespect toward her existing relationship.

Event: Sam stealth-proposes to Mercedes twice
Explanation: His repeated, partly covert proposals pursue his romantic goal while pressuring her and ignoring her boundaries.

Event: Sam joins the swimming team to impress Mercedes
Explanation: He improves himself and contributes athletically while giving Mercedes a non-harmful display of dedication.

Event: Sam puts Mercedes’ name in lights
Explanation: He invests effort and possibly resources to delight her, benefiting both with little downside.

Event: Sam refuses to take back the Valentine gift when Mercedes rejects him
Explanation: He accepts her decision yet leaves her with a gift, gaining some dignity while giving her an unencumbered present.

Event: Sam continues to look at Mercedes with obvious longing after ending his advances
Explanation: His lingering attachment hurts his own emotional recovery and may cause her discomfort without helping either of them.

Event: Mean jocks call Sam “Lady Lips”
Explanation: They gain amusement and dominance at notable emotional cost to him.

Event: Sue mistakes Sam for a girl and comments on getting “that girl” on her Cheerios
Explanation: She treats him as an object for her squad, asserting her agenda while disregarding his identity.

Event: Sam hides the fact that he bleaches his hair
Explanation: Concealing a trivial cosmetic secret protects his image without harming anyone.

Event: Sam confesses his hair-bleaching secret to Quinn when Kurt figures it out
Explanation: Admitting the truth relieves his anxiety and deepens trust with Quinn.

Event: Sam is more active in protecting his friends than Finn is
Explanation: His protective behavior benefits him through loyalty and purpose while clearly safeguarding others.

Event: Sam gives his word to sing a duet with Kurt
Explanation: He commits to support Kurt artistically despite potential social risk, strengthening both their positions.

Event: Finn advises Sam not to sing with Kurt to avoid bullying
Explanation: Finn gains no benefit and encourages fear-based self-limiting that harms their opportunities and Kurt’s support.

Event: Sam insists on keeping his word to Kurt despite Finn’s warning
Explanation: He upholds integrity and solidarity with Kurt, accepting risk to do what’s right for them both.

Event: Sam hides his inner dork and accepts insults to stay with Quinn
Explanation: He sacrifices authenticity and endures mistreatment for the relationship, giving Quinn a compliant boyfriend while harming himself.

Event: Sam hides his inner dork and accepts insults to stay with Santana
Explanation: He tolerates disrespect and self-erasure for her benefit, hurting his own well-being.

Event: Glee club members pool money to buy Sam’s guitar back
Explanation: They sacrifice their own resources to restore his property, strongly helping him at some personal cost.

Event: Sam cries when he sees they bought his guitar back
Explanation: His emotional response strengthens bonds and gratitude, rewarding the group socially.

Event: Sam gives a No-Holds-Barred beatdown to Karofsky defending Kurt, Mike, and Artie
Explanation: He risks punishment and injury but meaningfully protects bullied friends, offering them safety and solidarity.

Event: Sue repeatedly comments on Sam’s mouth size and appearance
Explanation: She treats him as an object for ridicule or desire, asserting power at his expense.

Event: Sam works as a stripper after moving to support his family
Explanation: He endures stigma but gains income and significantly helps his struggling parents.

Event: Sam becomes a walking shirtless scene for calendars, modeling, and playing Rocky
Explanation: He monetizes and leverages his assets while entertaining others, with no clear harm mentioned.

Event: Sam develops signature body rolls that spread to other club guys
Explanation: His moves enhance performances and influence peers’ style, benefiting everyone artistically.

Event: Sam dates five of the six girls in glee club in quick succession
Explanation: He avoids loneliness and gains repeated romantic fulfillment while creating emotional instability for others.

Event: Sam repeatedly falls into “epic romance” thinking and proposes twice
Explanation: His impulsive overcommitment undermines his own emotional stability and pressures partners without clear long-term gain.
